cursor.executemany("INSERT INTO items VALUES (?, ?, ?);", base_records)

# Enable WAL mode with exclusive lock
cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
cursor.execute("PRAGMA locking_mode=EXCLUSIVE;")
cursor.execute("PRAGMA wal_autocheckpoint=0;")

# Checkpoint to ensure base is clean
cursor.execute("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE);")
print(f"After checkpoint: {os.listdir('./fixtures/')}")

# Now add more records - these should go to WAL
cursor.execute("UPDATE items SET value = 89.8 WHERE id = 2;")
cursor.execute("UPDATE items SET value = 31.7 WHERE id = 3;")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (6, 'item6', 40.0);")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (7, 'item7', 30.0);")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (8, 'item8', 20.0);")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (9, 'item9', 10.0);")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (10, 'item10', 0.0);")
cursor.execute("INSERT INTO items VALUES (11, 'item11', -10.0);")

print(f"After inserts: {os.listdir('./fixtures/')}")

# Check WAL exists and corrupt it
if os.path.exists(wal_path):
    with open(wal_path, "rb") as f:
        wal_data = bytearray(f.read())
    
    print(f"WAL size: {len(wal_data)}")
    
    # Parse salts
    salt1 = struct.unpack(">I", wal_data[16:20])[0]
    salt2 = struct.unpack(">I", wal_data[20:24])[0]
    print(f"Original salts: {salt1:#010x}, {salt2:#010x}")
    
    # Corrupt header salts only
    wal_data[16:20] = struct.pack(">I", salt1 + 0x1234)
    wal_data[20:24] = struct.pack(">I", salt2 + 0x5678)
    
    with open(wal_path, "wb") as f:
        f.write(wal_data)
    print("WAL corrupted: header salts changed")

conn.close()
print(f"After close: {os.listdir('./fixtures/')}")
